zh.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III是Ralph Fitz Stephens II和Matilda de Calz的兒子，於大約1122年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾。他於1185年與Cecily Berkeley在英國波克夏結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於大約1190年在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾文特爾布恩享年70歲，埋葬在英國格盧斯特斯爾邦藍。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III是Ralph Fitz Stephens II和Matilda de Calz的儿子，于大约1122年出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾。他于1185年与Cecily Berkeley在英国波克夏结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于大约1190年在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾文特爾布恩享年70岁，埋葬在英国格盧斯特斯爾邦藍。

nameMeanings.name: Fitz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

variants.name: Fitzgerald
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Brendan, Eamon, Liam, Aidan, Bridgid, Caitlin, Donal, Fitz, Fitzgerald, Ciaran, Colum, Dermot.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gerald (see Gerald ), ‘son of Gerald’. This is a very common Anglo-Norman name in Ireland, where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Gearailt. Irish bearers of the name claim descent from Gerald Fitzwalter de Windsor, constable of Pembroke Castle in the early 12th century, whose grandson Raymond (or Redmond) ‘le Gros’ FitzGerald (died after 1185) led the invasion of Ireland on behalf of Strongbow in 1170. A branch of the family held the title Knight of Glin from at least 1299–2011.

variants.name: Fitzwater
meanings: Irish: altered form of Fitzwalter, a patronymic from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Walter , meaning ‘son of Walter’.

History: This is the name of a family of Norman origin, descended from Godfrey, Count of Brionne (died c. 1010). Godfrey's grandson, Richard FitzGilbert, accompanied William the Conqueror and was made Lord of Clare. His great-grandson was Robert FitzWalter, one of the barons who guaranteed the Magna Carta in 1215.

variants.name: Fitzherbert
meanings: English and Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Herbert (see Herbert ), ‘son of Herbert’.

variants.name: Fitzhenry
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Henry (see Henry ), ‘son of Henry’. This surname is common in Ireland (mainly Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as Mac Éinrí. In at least some cases, it seems to have been adopted as a surname indicating the illegitimate offspring of a man called Henry.

variants.name: Fitzsimmons
meanings: Irish and English: variant of Fitzsimon, with post-medieval excrescent -s, from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + either of the personal names Simon or Sigmund (see Simon ), ‘son of Simon or Sigmund’. The name is also found in the Gaelicized form Mac Síomóin.

variants.name: Fitzgibbon
meanings: Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Gibbon, a Norman French pet form of Gilbert ; ‘son of Gibbon’. This name has long been a widespread and important surname in Ireland, and Gaelicized as Mac Giobúin. Sir Gilbert (Gibbon) FitzJohn, illegitimate son of John FitzGerald, 1st Baron Desmond (died 1261), was the ancestor of a line of Anglo-Norman aristocracy in Ireland, who acquired the surname Fitzgibbon with reference to him. Gilbert and his Fitzgibbon descendants were known as ‘the White Knights’ on account of their fair complexion.

variants.name: Fitzharris
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Harry ‘son of Harry’. MacLysaght describes this as the name of a distinguished County Wexford family, also known as Fitzhenry .

variants.name: Fitzmaurice
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aidan, Ciaran, Colm.

Irish: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Maurice (see Morris 1), ‘son of Maurice’. This surname is common in Ireland, where it has also been Gaelicized as Mac Muiris. The Irish family of Fitzmaurice share a common Norman ancestry with the Fitzgerald family. They can be traced to Thomas Fitzmaurice, whose brother Gerald was the ancestor of the Irish Fitzgerald dynasty. Their father was Maurice Fitzgerald, a Norman baron (died 1176).

variants.name: Fitzhugh
meanings: English (Northamptonshire): from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (see Fitz ) + the personal name Hugh (see Hugh ), ‘son of Hugh’.

History: William Fitzhugh (1651–1701), from Bedford, England, emigrated to VA c. 1670 and established himself on the Potomac River in what was then Stafford County, VA, as a planter and exporter. He also practiced law, was a member of the Virginia House of Burgesses, and served in 1687 as lieutenant colonel of the county militia.

variants.name: Fitzroy
meanings: English: from Anglo-Norman French fi(t)z ‘son’ (from Latin filius), used originally to distinguish a son from a father bearing the same personal name and then conventionalized as a hereditary surname, or short for a surname of which fiz ‘son’ was the first element, for example Fitzhenry and Fitzroy .
Altered form of English   Fitch  .
German: from a vernacular pet form of the personal name Vinzenz (see Vincent ) or Vitus (see Vito , compare Veit ). Compare also Fietz .

nameMeanings.name: Stephen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: from the personal name Stephen, a vernacular form of Biblical Latin Stephanus, Greek Stephanos (from Greek stephanos ‘crown’). The modern English form is also spelled Steven ; the -ph- spelling is due to medieval clerks' knowledge of the Biblical Latin form.

variants.name: Santisteban
meanings: Spanish: habitational name from any of numerous places called Santisteban or Santesteban (from the Latin genitive form Sancti Stephani) for a local church or shrine dedicated to Saint Stephen (Spanish San Esteban); see Stephen . Compare Santiesteban and Santistevan .

variants.name: Steven
meanings: Scottish, English, Dutch, and North German: from the personal name Steven, from Latin Stephanus, Greek Stephanos ‘crown’. This was a popular name throughout Christendom in the Middle Ages, having been borne by the first Christian martyr, stoned to death at Jerusalem three years after the death of Christ. In North America, this surname has also absorbed various cognates from other languages, e.g. German Steffen , German and Polish Stefan , Slovenian and Slovak Štefan, Hungarian István (see Istvan ), and also their patronymics and other derivatives (see Stevens ).

nameMeanings.name: Ralph
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

variants.name: Ralf
meanings: From a Norman French name, Raulf, a contracted form of the Germanic personal name Radulf, derived from rād ‘counsel’ + wulf ‘wolf’. The spelling with -ph is due to classical influence in the 18th century.

en.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III was born about 1122, in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom as the son of Ralph Fitz Stephens II and Matilda de Calz. He married Cecily Berkeley in 1185, in Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died about 1190, in Winterbourne, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 70, and was buried in Buckland, Gloucestershire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III est né environ 1122, à Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Ralph Fitz Stephens II et Matilda de Calz. Il a épousé Cecily Berkeley en 1185, à Berkshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé environ 1190, à Winterbourne, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 70 ans, et a été enterré à Buckland, Gloucestershire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III wurde um 1122 in Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Ralph Fitz Stephens II und Matilda de Calz geboren. Er hat 1185 in Berkshire, England Cecily Berkeley geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb um 1190 im Alter von 70 Jahren in Winterbourne, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Buckland, Gloucestershire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III è nato il circa 1122 nel Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Ralph Fitz Stephens II e Matilda de Calz. Lui sposato Cecily Berkeley nel 1185, nel Berkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì circa 1190, nel Winterbourne, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 70 anni, e fu sepolto a Buckland, Gloucestershire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen IIIは約1122年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアでRalph Fitz Stephens IIとMatilda de Calzの息子として生まれた。1185年にイギリスバークシュアイアでCecily Berkeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は約1190年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアウィンテルボウルンで70歳で死去し，イギリスグロウセステルシュアイアブックランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III(은)는 대략 1122년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르에서 Ralph Fitz Stephens II(와)과 Matilda de Calz의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1185년 영국 버크실르에서 Cecily Berkeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 대략 1190년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 윈터보우른에서 70세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 그루스스터실르 벅랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III nasceu aproximadamente 1122, em Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Ralph Fitz Stephens II e Matilda de Calz. Ele casou-se com Cecily Berkeley em 1185, em Berkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 1190, em Winterbourne, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 70 anos, e foi sepultado em Buckland, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III родился около 1122 г. в местности глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. Его родителей звали Ralph Fitz Stephens II и Matilda de Calz. Он женился на женщине по имени Cecily Berkeley в 1185 г. в местности биркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер около 1190 г. в местности винтирбурн, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 70 лет и был похоронен в местности буккланд, глоусестиршир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Lord Ralph Fitz Stephen III nació aproximadamente 1122, en Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Ralph Fitz Stephens II y Matilda de Calz. Se casó con Cecily Berkeley en 1185, en Berkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió aproximadamente 1190, en Winterbourne, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 70 años, y fue sepultado en Buckland, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra.
